The Lost Fortress of Mekshir guarded the trade routes from Osirion to its province of Thuvia until it was buried in a sandstorm during the khamsin season of -841 AR. The fortress is rumored to contain treasures including an armory of magical weapons and several months of payroll, but is said to be guarded by the undead.

Lost Lane is a street in Rag's End, one of Magnimar's poorest neighborhoods in the city's Beacon's Point district. It is also the location of the headquarters of Magnimar's premier thieves' guild: the Night Scales.

Lost Lodge is a secret but forgotten Desnan safehouse in Nidal's southeastern Uskwood Forest that contains supplies, weapons, and relics of the Song of Spheres, but is surrounded by a nest of deathwebs, placed there by Shades of the Uskwood druids centuries ago.

The Lost Mine of Siwat is a lost silver mine located in the Sahure Wastes of central Osirion. It was originally founded in 4349 AR, but mysteriously disappeared only a few years thereafter. Few people today even remember that the silver mine, and the small village of Siwat that grew up around it, ever existed.

The Mapstone Monument is in the Lowcleft district of Magnimar in the Seerspring Gardens. This block of marble has been carved into a scale model of the city. The church of Abadar updates the sculpture annually using spells. On some holidays, other illusion spells are used for appealing effects. Meditating here is reputed to increase your mental resistance.
The Merry Pranksters is a group of two dozen pirates who survive on the island of Hesmene's Grotto in the Shackles.

Mirnura was once the capital of Jistka Imperium, founded by the legendary hero Arustun. The city was named after Arustun's beloved wife. It was situated on what is now the northern coast of Rahadoum, on the Fallen Peninsula north of the Path of Salt.

Mirnura was the main target in Ancient Osirion's campaign to destroy the Jistkan empire. When Osirians burned the city to the ground, they used millions of tons of salt to eradicate all signs of life around it. The Path of Salt region was named for this epic razing.

Powerful sandstorms sometimes reveal hints of monuments, and the vaults of Jistkan emperors are thought to show themselves to individuals of royal Jistkan descent. One of these vaults, known among the treasure hunters as the Halite Crypt, lies in tremendous depths and is rumored to still be protected by a Jistkan behemoth golem by the name Helatoros.

The Mistress of Angels is a fine marble sculpture located in Magnimar's southern Ordellia district. It depicts Ordellia Whilwren, one of Magnimar's founders, scanning the horizon of the Varisian Gulf, seemingly looking out for danger. Whilwren was a cleric of Desna and one of Alcaydian Indros' colleagues at the founding of the city, and the statue is an occasional gathering place for Magnimarian Mystery Cultists.
Mistshroud Cliff is in the eastern reaches of the Eismonts mountain range, south of the Maiestas River in southwestern Cheliax.
Mistwatch is a semi-ruined stone castle located atop a granite cliff overlooking the Verduran Fork east of the Isle of Arenway, in the Verduran Province of Taldor.
Moak Harbor was a port city on Shark Island in the Shackles that was home to more than 8,000 people at its peak more than 200 years ago, but today is only rotting ruins.

From the Shoanti term meaning "steps into hell," this perfectly circular pit is located at the base of the Kodar Mountains, on the Storval Plateau of northern Varisia, approximately 25 miles northeast of the source of the Aloren River. A set of spiral stairs run around the edge of the hole, but ends after only a few hundred feet. Beyond this point it is necessary to climb the sheer walls or fly. Few people know how deep the Mobhad Leigh actually is, although some rumor that it is so deep that it bypasses the Darklands region of Nar-Voth completely and opens directly into Sekamina.

Mohgan, also called the City of the Lakes, was a settlement in the kingdom of Gastash, north of its capital. The city was chosen by Zutha to investigate a qlippoth runestone associated with Oaur-Ooung, which he had discovered in the Astral Plane.

Zutha planned to use the powerful stone to create an artifact that would grant infinite necromantic energy, but when his apprentices awakened the runestone's power, a wave of black liquid began to emerge from it. Zutha and his assistants created an anti-magic containment field capable of holding back the flood for some time, and before they could develop a long-term plan to solve the problem, Earthfall destroyed his kingdom. Since then, the city has been forgotten, but little by little, the barriers blocking the water are eroding, and the surrounding region, called the Keening Hills, is teeming with life, with lakes and creatures of all kinds. For their part, the orcs avoid the area completely.

Located in central Mendev, the Monastery of Tala caters to many faiths of the crusaders going to fight the demons in the Worldwound. Run by a cloistered order of monks from numerous faiths named the Brotherhood of Tala, it was founded in 4702 AR as a place where the various churches could conduct research on how best to combat the demonic forces of the Worldwound.
The Monastery of the Unremitting Tide is historically an Iroran monastery on the eastern coast of Nex.
The Monastery of the Veil is home to an isolated and mysterious group of monks. It is located in the remote Hungry Mountains in the eastern reaches of Ulcazar county in Ustalav.

The Monkey's Paw is a 60-foot-tall statue in the shape of a simian forearm and six-fingered hand located on the northern shores of Lake Ocota in the Mwangi Expanse. The tips of each of the fingers is shaped like the face of a different species of ape, cleverly hidden in the folds of the fingerprints. The eyes of the apes seem to follow one around as one moves around the statue. Ancient glyphs surround the base of the statue that are written in an ancient hybrid of the Chthonian and Mwangi languages. A secret door in the statue leads one into a small religious shrine dedicated to the demon lord Angazhan.

The Monolith Glacier is located near the north-westernmost corner of the Thanelands in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings; it is formed in the western reaches of the high Stormspear Mountains range and is slowly moving southwards. The Monolith Glacier has a distinct, very symmetrical oblong shape and those few brave explorers who have ventured up onto the glacier report seeing a large fortress entombed within the very heart of the glacier. Monolith Glacier is a very dangerous place to explore: blizzards often engulf it; its surface is riddled with crevasses and ice tunnels created by the numerous deadly remorhazes that live in the glacier's depths. Unbeknownst to everyone, the glacier is a sentient elemental that speaks with a voice of grinding ice and might be willing to allow access to the strange structure at its heart to anyone who can rid it of the pesky remorhazes infesting it.

The Moon's Eye is a black granite formation located on the Nesmian Plains between the Deepcut River and the Marideth River. The formation has some religious significance to Kellid tribes in the area, dating back centuries. Explorers of the region occasionally report finding Kellid ruins in the area surrounding the Moon's Eye, though all reports are inconsistent of the location and description of these ruins. There are reports of unusually large wolves in the area as well.
The Moonless Mirror is a relic of the Everwar with Cheliax, a sealed portal to the Netherworld that sits in the cliffs overlooking Nisroch Bay and seethes with magical energy.
The Moonpool Causeways are a series of magical pools ranging in width from a few feet to half a mile located in the northeastern Fierani Forest of Kyonin.

Moonwing's Bough is a small pocket of oak trees in the evergreen Grungir Forest which hosts an ancient shrine to Desna built thousands of years ago, during the reign of the Thassilonian Empire. The shrine is one of the most sacred sites of Desna's faith but very few worshipers attempt to visit it, traveling through the dangerous Grungir Forest. Moonwing's Bough is named after the first priest of Desna who reached the shrine in the modern age. The shrine is preserved in very good condition.

properties.labels: Morilla Palace and the Tally Wall

properties.text:
Mount Antios is Taldor's third-highest point. It is a snow-covered craggy peak of the World's Edge Mountains that stands tall above the south-central Tandak Plains. It is named after Taldor's tenth emperor who constructed an enormous burial site there known as Antios's Crown, including many huge statues representing himself, as well as statues of eagles, lions, and statues of the ancient gods of Old Taldor.

Mount Antios is located about 150 miles northeast of Oppara.

Mount Carissa is the seventh-tallest peak of the Five Kings Range at 15,126 feet. It is one of four mountains surrounding the city of Taggoret, alongside Mount Onik to its east, Mount Langley to its north, and Mount Soryu to its west.

Mount Osiki is the tallest mountain within the range of Brazen Peaks in northern Garund, east of the Pahmet settlement of Erekrus. Its foothills and smaller peaks extend almost to the Crook River.

An ancient stairway begins from a stone landing on the southern bank of the Crook, winding through the foothills and the peaks to end at the top of the mountain. Upon the peak is an ancient shrine to a forgotten deity, and the few people that climb the ancient stairway each year are rumored to have committed to the pilgrimage after being saved from death or ruin by a dark-cloaked stranger. Nothing is known about what happens at the shrine, as the pilgrims returning from the top of Mount Osiki do not talk about it under any inducement.
Mount Rein is a large, snow-topped mountain in the western end of the Fog Peaks, on the border between the nations of Galt and Taldor. It is used as a guide to the border between those two nations.

Mount Rinia is a 15,000-foot-tall peak in the western Menador Mountains, located in the Archduchy of Menador in Cheliax. It is the Maiestas River's source, and home to orcs, humans, and yrthaks.

The mountain is named for the subject of a Taldan ballad, a noblewoman who flung herself from a mountain to escape an arranged marriage, but sprouted wings and flew away to unknown freedom.

Mount Sinatuk is a volcano at the far western end of the Five Kings Range, near the border of Druma and Isger. It is approximately two miles tall.

While the surrounding lands contain large fragments of black volcanic rock as evidence of an ancient eruption, as of 4712 AR the volcano had not been geographically active during the recorded history of the dwarves of the Five Kings Mountains. The nearby independent dwarven Regulate of Grimsburrow had used the dormant volcano's geothermic properties to provide heat, light, and steam power, and discovered mineable diamonds deep within its vacant lava tubes.

Mount Yogan is a volcano in southern Minkai along the Kyojin Mountains, near the source of the Sakehiyaku River. It is one of the Three Fires, along with Mount Kumijinja in the Kamifushi Mountains and Mount Kazunan in the central Kyojin Mountains.

The Mount of Black Fire is an extraordinary mountain in the Tusk Mountains. Unlike other active volcanoes, this mountain spews forth a dark ebony flame. Even more strangely, this ebony fire puts out regular fire. This unstable fire comes from overlapping gates connected to the Plane of Fire and the Void inside the mountain.

Of particular interest is the attraction of the Blackfire Adepts, a group of powerful individuals associated with an arcane cult. The Blackfire Adepts are drawn to the Mount of Black Fire due to its connection to distant planes and the eldritch energies it possesses. However, despite their efforts, they have not yet discovered a means to fully master or control this unique and enigmatic power source.

The Mourning Burrows were once a mass burial ground for Kellids who lived in Druma long ago. Today, Razmiran insurgents who survived their failed raid on Twingate have settled in the interconnected tunnels between the many mounds here.

Found in a desolate valley at the foot of the Mindspin Mountains in north-eastern Varisia, the Obelisk Forest of Mundatei is a ruin from the time of the Thassilonian empire and was built with the labor of enslaved rune and stone giants. Thousands of ten-foot-high stones, some carved with runes, are scattered about the landscape, and it has long been rumored that they are hollow and hold treasure. A century ago, an expedition from Korvosa tried to confirm this by breaking open some of them. They found that the obelisks were indeed hollow, each holding the desiccated body of a human who had died in terror and horrific pain. According to the Korvosan survivors, they were assaulted by strange undead that night who dragged off a dozen of their companions. The next morning it was discovered that the broken obelisks had wholly reformed. The site has been avoided ever since.

Murkfen is a swamp south of the Hymbrian Forest that is the domain of a feared mobogo named Crapaudine. Travelers and hunters avoid the area to avoid her wrath. A tribe of boggards serves the mobogo by capturing people and sacrificing them in her name.

The Mystery of the Gate is an impressive inn and tavern in the Leeward District of Riddleport. It is favored by sages and scholars who visit the city to study the Cyphergate, and also increasingly patronized by adventurers exploring Varisia and the lands beyond. Its walls are decorated by glyphs and diagrams of the ancient monument, and the inn hosts twice-a-week lectures from cyphermages of the Order of Cyphers.

Mystic's Redoubt is the only public arcane library in Port Peril. It is a six-winged circular tower in Crescent Harbor, and it charges fees of as much as 1,000 gold pieces per year for its use. A small number of apprentices join the library each year, while other scholars visit Mystic's Redoubt to study relics in its antiquities hall. Rare natural components also grow in an adjacent, well-maintained garden.
Naldak's Point is an abandoned dwarven town near Dalun in Irrisen.

The Nameless Spires are the ruins of an ancient city located on Golarion's geographical North Pole, in the Crown of the World. The city was built by the saumen kar after the formation of Golarion but before Earthfall.

Nargrym's Tomb is the resting place of the legendary giantslayer Nargrym Steelhand. The tomb was built north of Janderhoff in a forest in the Mindspin Mountains and has been forgotten due to the treacherous actions of the giantslayer's companion, leading to the belief that it is haunted.

The ruined city of Narona is located on Hellmouth Gulf on the western coast of Cheliax on the Arcadian Ocean. The city was destroyed by the Chelaxian Navy because of its resistance against the rule of House Thrune. Unlike the inhabitants of the nearby city of Pezzack, Narona's residents never returned after the settlement's destruction.

The natatorium of Nal-Kashel, an island in the Hellmouth Gulf west of Cheliax off the shore of Blackcove, was a stepped public pool once used by the island's Azlanti inhabitants during the era of ancient Azlant. Like the rest of the abandoned island, it is now in ruins.

Nature's Glass is a small, natural pool of water deep within the Fangwood near Crossfen. Its surface reflects like a mirror, and a legend has grown up around it that the water reflects the true nature of those who gaze into it. This phenomenon has attracted the attention of some fey, among them Leeta, a Swan maiden determined to protect the waters by drowning those who try to poison them.

Navah's Tower is a structure built into the side of a cliff located 20 miles from Longshadow in the Hollow Hills. Navah created the tower using transmutation spells, and the area radiates this type of magic. Another tower and a basement accompany the main structure, which is well-positioned for defense.

The ruins of the town of Nazilli are located on the southern edges of the Verduran Forest in Taldor. The settlement was destroyed by the black dragon Aeteperax during a period of Taldan history known as the Dragon Plague.

The Ndele Gap is a high-altitude mountain pass in western Nex between the Barrier Wall to the north and the Shattered Range to the south.

It is the most obvious and frequently travelled eastern route into the Mwangi Expanse, and Taldor's ill-fated Sixth Army of Exploration marched through the Gap on its way to disaster in the jungle.

The thin air and cold temperatures make travel difficult, and travellers are occasionally attacked by bandits and kholo.
New Krane is the fortress headquarters of the Hellknight Order of the Coil and is located in Sargava on the shore of Desperation Bay, northeast of the capital city of Eleder.

The Newt Market is located in Kintargo's scholastic district of Villegre and serves as both the district's main marketplace and a draw for adventurers. The market is known for selling a surprisingly large amount of rare goods and magic items, including a wand of greater invisibility.

The Newt Market is maintained by an organiser known as The Newt, a title that changes hands on a regular basis but by 4724 AR had been held for an uncharacteristically long time by a charismatic gnome alchemist who happily uses bribery and intimidation to protect his interests.

Nicyruse was an ancient city of the Jistka Imperium located today in southern Avistan, on the Inner Sea coast. It housed one of the largest temple-forges in all the Imperium, rivaling even that of Rachikan. When Jistka's doom became imminent, divine earthquakes tore the region apart and city's forges were abandoned. Now the seaside ruins of Nicyruse have mostly eroded away by the Inner Sea.

In 4712 AR, reports from the Chelaxian city of Hinji to the east stated that a recent storm had cleared a new entrance to the buried site. Fisherfolk said that mechanical creatures began moving across the shore near the ruins, grabbing fish from the ocean with their bare hands, and taking them back into the ruins. The Pathfinder Society has called for adventurers to explore the ruins and bring back any information about lost Jistka.
The presence of the Nidalese Embassy in the Castle District of Kintargo is unsurprising given the city's proximity to the shadowy nation of Nidal and how much trade comes from these neighbouring.

Nightbinder's Wharf is a fortress located where the Usk River flows into Nisroch Bay just to the south of Nisroch. Shadowcallers are dispatched here for missions abroad, where their mastery of shadows makes them invaluable to dignitaries from various countries. Most of those who hire the shadowcallers are Chelaxian, though private companies also gather here, even though such activities are prohibited in their home countries. Discretion is paramount, and thus Nightbinder's Wharf is shrouded in shadow magic; those unfortunate enough to stumble upon it often vanish.

Nightfall Station is the most profitable of the Aspis Consortium trading posts in the Mwangi Expanse. It is thirteen days' travel by wheelboat against the current from Bloodcove (assuming the travelers push on for a while after dark on the last day and arrive at the station at night, hence the name) but the return journey with the current is only five days.

As of 4710 AR, a plant called nightmoss that grows in towering cypress trees throughout the Embeth Forest was in high demand. A small number of Uringen residents use their secret formulae to brew the moss into powerful potions that strengthen the body and heal physical conditions. A human druid named Erinin Thulgath stole the secret of nightmoss's healing properties and fled to establish Nightmoss Camp at the southeastern edge of the forest, where Thulgath and her group brew potent alchemical products.

The Nightways Gate and the Night Road are named for linking Kintargo to its shadowy northern neighbour of Nidal via the scholastic district of Villegre. The gate and road form an awkward but necessary trade route, as due to Kintargo's distance from Cheliax's central heartlands, goods from Nidal are often cheaper.

When Kintargo was under Chelaxian rule, this route also granted the fell influence of Nidalese culture another inroad into Cheliax. After Kintargo and Ravounel won their independence from Cheliax in 4716 AR, Lord Mayor Jilia Bainilus has engaged in active diplomacy with Nidal.

Nirodin Villa is a stately townhouse in the northwest of Magnimar's Alabaster District. Situated directly next to Kaddren Villa, it is the seat of House Nirodin, and run by Cheiskaia Nirodin, the family's matriarch.

The No-Horn is a low quality tavern in Magnimar's Dockway neighbourhood. It typifies the sort of establishment that pops up periodically near the Bazaar of Sails, only to close again in short order. It is named for its sign, which depicts a hornless unicorn being tormented by drunken devils.

The structure itself is a rickety and leaning thing constructed from an old ship's hull. The establishment's barkeep is an ornery man missing both of his ears. Patrons can expect cheap food and drink here, but both are of an extraordinarily low quality.

The Nogortha Necropolis (also known as the Necropolis of Nogortha) is a vast burial site located in central Andoran between the Arthfell Mountains and Nogortha Peaks, southwest of the city of Oregent. These barrow mounds are the final resting place of hundreds of barbarians who once inhabited the Arthfell Forest but who were finally defeated in 1707 AR by Taldor's campaign of western expansion led by General Khastalus. Many of the burial mounds have been emptied by grave robbers or are being excavated by Pathfinders, although many also remain to be explored.

Norboro was the second town built on the cursed island of Whyrlis Rock, in the Shackles. It was founded only 6 months after its sister town of Heskott Bay. At about the same time that the residents of Heskott Bay vanished into thin air, the town of Norboro turned to ash. The location is now known as the Norboro Ashes.

The Nordein Glacier is located in the central Fog Peaks of southern Galt. The mountain on which the glacier sits contains a hidden valley that is slowly being swallowed by the ice. Legends claim that an evil spirit called the Jarl of the North Wind lives near the glacier.

Nystra is a ghost-town in the far-eastern reaches of the River Kingdoms. Once a thriving community centered around the cultivation and production of the fine silk produced by the insectoid silk gargoyles, or silkgoyles, its inhabitants were totally wiped out in a revenge plot by the Taldan druid Tharl Grimull in 4664 AR.

Oagon was a city in the now-lost kingdom of Lirgen. Its location at the mouth of the Black Flow river (in what is now known as the Sodden Lands) ensured it was completely covered by floodwater on the advent of the Eye of Abendego in 4606 AR.

Oakrib Inn is the site where the Silver Ravens negotiated the details of Ravounel's secession from Cheliax. This large inn near the head of the Yolubilis River was once a popular stopping point for those traveling through the Menador Gap. Since the destruction of Menador Keep and the closure of the Gap, the inn's owner Olradi Ormovor has reinvented it as a luxury tourist destination and historical heritage site, complete with dramatic reenactments of the tense negotiations.

The Osprey is one of the ten remaining pilings of the Irespan in Magnimar. It is the shortest of the ten, standing only 100 feet high.

There is an entrance into the upper piling. The church of Abadar is rumored to operate a prison here. Locals believe that political prisoners and others that are best hidden away are held here.

The Osprey Club, located in the Naos district of Magnimar, caters to a restrictive clientele of the richest families in the city. Gambling, drinking, and smoking are common activities here; there is also a brothel, for those so inclined. The exterior of the club is guarded by an iron fence to keep the onlookers outside. Green and gold magical lights illuminate the outside every night. The club is currently run by Kayasi Zivatchi.
Pergamon Pass is a route through the south of the World's Edge Mountains in Taldor. The closest settlement to the pass is the town of Dalaston.
Petronax Estate is the Petronax family home, made up of three multi-story structures. Vayde Petronax, the family patriarch, rarely leaves this estate.

Pho Yim, sometimes written Mount Pho-Yim, is an enormous volcano located in the south of Tang Mai that looms over the nation's capital of Surat Prakan and is a part of the vast Kullan Dei mountain range. While Pho Yim is currently not active, it does smoke ominously and it is rumoured that its molten fury is only held at bay by the harmony of the five sorcerous rulers of Tang Mai. Nevertheless, During the Age of Ascendancy, the volcano did erupt explosively destroying much of Tang Mai in 7099 IC and, some say, in consequence of the invasion of the country by Lung Wa some years previously.

The local hero Ankilhi used the fires of Pho Yim to forge a tetsubo out of the remains of the graveknight Pnao Kilnak.

The Pidderack Shaft is a mysterious vertical shaft found on the Nesmian Plains. Named for the family of farmers who discovered it when digging a new well, there is no established depth to the shaft, and the worked stone that lines the shaft gives no hints to its history. On the winter solstice, unusually large ghostly spiders emerge from the shaft. The farmhouse that surrounds the shaft has long been abandoned.

The Pig is a run-down tavern in the working-class Rag's End neighborhood of Magnimar's Beacon's Point district. A former slaughterhouse, the building housing the Pig had been condemned for years before being converted into a drinking establishment. Even though the wind whistles through its many holes and leaks, it has never been able to completely rid itself of its abattoir smell.

The Pig is patronized mostly by butchers who work nearby and who are used to the smell. It serves few drinks and even less food, but is known for brineskin curls, a pickled pig-skin dish soaked in brine and blood, that can turn the stomach of even the most veteran butcher.

The Pillar of Palamia is an ancient obelisk made from basalt believed to be a monument to a forgotten god in the northwestern Whisperwood of central Cheliax. Others claim that it is a god's prison, and that the Pillar sticking into the ground is itself the nail holding in the trapped deity.

The Pillars of Kreth are located on the plains just west of the Flood Road in northern Belkzen. The Pillars are a series of eleven dolmens—massive vertical standing stones topped with flat lintels. The site was discovered by the orc Kreth but the orcs are superstitious by nature and they have never revisited the Pillars of Kreth since then. The first ten dolmens are each between 8 and 9 feet in height, 3 feet wide and 2 feet thick. The linels are 3 feet thick, overhanging the stones beneath them by approximately a foot on all sides. The easternmost dolmen is only around 4 feet high. The stones seem to have been worked by primitive hand tools and contain numerous symbols and pictograms that correspond to no known language. Some of the symbols are clearly stars, comets, and planets. The eleven dolmens forms an irregular tunnel that once a year aligns with the red planet Akiton. During this time the sky above the Pillars turns strange colors, and a vibration can be felt from half a mile away. As the vibrations become stronger, a torus of churning auroral light rises a mile above the site and a pulse of energy passes through the first ten dolmens and strikes the top of the easternmost one. Anything on top of the last dolmen dissapears for ever.

Pathfinders who have studied the Pillars of Kreth believe that they could be a kind of interplanetary transportation device, similar to the Doorway to the Red Star in the Mwangi Expanse or the Maze of the Open Road in Galt.
The Pillars of Rovagug are a set of mountains in the far north of the World's Edge Mountains in Taldor.

The Pit of Zedoran is a vast sinkhole within the depths of the Northern Fangwood. Streams of water carrying leaves and organic debris cascade down its sides, and layers of moss and lichen cover hanging vines and ancient roots. The thick forest canopy allows little sunlight to reach the moss-covered earth.

The pit is named for the green dragon Zedoran who as of 4722 AR still laired here. At the edge of the central pit is a wooden cage lift, manually operated by Zedoran's minions. There are two cages, one at the top and the other at the bottom of the pit, each large enough to hold eight humans.

Sabkha is a shallow basin filled with gritty salt-laden sand. It is the primary source for the nagri salt that farmers use to make pesh. Farmers travel to Sabkha once a season to sift salt out of the dirt and carry sacks of it home. Merchants flock to Sabkha to collect nagri salt, which they then sell in the markets of Katapesh. Once a month or so, heavy rains fill Sabkha's shallow basin with trapped rainwater. After several days, the water drains out through the rock, leaving new salt deposits on the surface.

Sable Barrow is a hill that overlooks the Numerian city of Chesed. The Pathfinder Gojan the Sharp observed a golden statue appear on the Barrow the night before a seemingly inevitable slave revolt. The statue hefted a mighty hammer and seemed to be threatening the city with it, and carried the mark of the god Abadar on his chest armor. Gojan believed that the statue's presence prevented the slave revolt, and it disappeared four days later. Most agree that the golden statue was actually the Lawgiver, the herald of Abadar.

Lawgiver's appearance instead inspired the slaves to join with the city's defenders in order to repel an unprecedented, and since unequaled, assault upon the city by barbarians. After the city's successful defense against the raid, Chesed abolished its slave trade, emancipated its slaves, and decreed that any slave brought into the city would be declared free within an hour of arrival if not registered with the city's government.

The Sable Sands are a desolate location in the southeastern deserts of Katapesh that is avoided by most travellers; the only people who visit are the occasional lost travellers who mistake it for a distant oasis.

The Sable Sands are about 100 feet in diameter and filled with a tarry, black substance that covers the ground in strange patterns. From the midst of the tar protrude several weather-worn tombstones, though no one knows who or what may have been buried beneath the sable sands.

The tar that permeates the ground seems to suck the happiness and positivity out of all who come near it, leeching the warmth from the very air itself. Though no undead have ever crawled from the tar-filled graves, the area does seem to attract them, drawing all sorts of living dead toward this desolate site.
The Salmon is one of the ten remaining pilings of the Irespan in Magnimar. It stands 220 feet high. There are no known entrances into the piling.

Once the busiest gate in the city, the Salt Gate is now a useless relic. The internal workings of the gate have rusted so much that the gate can no longer be used and is stuck open. The Salt Gate's usage declined as a growing amount of shipping moved from nearby Old Harbor to Yolubilis Harbor and the Foreign Quarter of Jarvis End, but it is still used by the traders of Salt Market.

The Salt Market, despite its misleading name, mainly deals in building supplies and it is the first port of call for Kintargo's architects and builders. Its location in Old Kintargo just inside the Salt Gate gives it easy access to the Yolubilis River from which much of its goods are delivered.
Sampadabad is a ruined city in the north-west of Vudra's storied central region known as the Cradle. Sampadabad was not a Vudrani city but rather one built by nagas.

The Sanctum of Sand is a mysterious location in the desert of southern Katapesh. It is believed to be located inside a valley within the Trackless Storm and is the origin of the Decanter of Black Breath artifact.
Sanctum of the Sorrowmaker is a labyrinthine cavern complex in Dahak's Fang island that Aashaq the Annihilator and her minions call home.

Sanctum's Watch was built to guard a pass in the Southern Range of the World's Edge Mountains in the Taldan province of Ligos, a purpose which it no longer serves. Sanctum's Watch is a fortress expertly hidden amongst the mountains and the valley itself was a sight of much bloodshed in the early days of the Grand Campaign, when the Taldan army faced off against the forces of Qadira. Seen as an easy way to march an army into the soft belly of Taldor, the valley was eventually fortified, which discouraged Qadira from ever trying this approach again and led to disuse. After several centuries Sanctum's Watch was all but abandoned, with Taldan officials even forgetting how the hidden fortress was accessed.

Sangoshi was a metropolis on an island off the southeastern cost of Minkai before being overtaken by the Okaiyo Ocean hundreds of years ago. Its ruins are now a coral reef miles off the eastern shore of modern Minkai. Tales suggest the ruins rise from the depths for one day every 44 years.

The cave-pocked Saresp Mountain, a peak in Mendev's far eastern reaches, was once the site of many ancient tombs as well as shelters for families seeking refuge from the harshest parts of winter. The caves hosted many Sarkorian refugees after the opening of the Worldwound, but all disappeared near the end of the Mendevian Crusades, their caves either sealed or apparently struck by grave robbers.
Sarini Estate, located in the Greens district of Kintargo, Ravounel, is the home of House Sarini. Most of its Thrune-loyalist residents fled Kintargo after the Kintargan Rebellion.

Scar Thicket is a forbidding overgrown area deep in the namesake Scarhusrt region of the Whisperwood of Cheliax, tucked between the flows of the Doracium, Malvesa, and Iseld rivers.

Infrequently visited because of its evil reputation, this area remains a primal woodland and frequently features in hushed conversations and outlandish stories. Everything from monstrous [[insect]s, deformed animals, and enormous bats are rumored to emerge from its depths. Youths in the area grow up learning to deal with evil outsiders, and the inhabitants are quite skilled at leading hunts for them.
Schloss Caromarc is an abandoned castle in the northeast of the Ustalavic county of Vieland, and is the former residence of Vieland's ex-ruler, Count Alpon Caromarc.

Seraph's Ladder is a freestanding golden and white marble ladder that stands on the Blood Plains of the Hold of Belkzen. It is believed that Seraph's Ladder is one of the few remnants of the Thassilonian domain of Gastash, dating from the reign of Zutha, Runelord of Gluttony.
Serenity's Copse is a community of dryads who live in a grove deep in Cheliax's Anferita Wood.

Serpent's Run is the largest building in Magnimar, quite a feat in the so-called city of monuments, and is located in one of the city's most prosperous areas, the Marble District. Serpent's Run is Magnimar's arena and most popular entertainment venue; the hippodrome can seat up to 5,000 people, over a quarter of Magnimar's total population. Unlike many other arenas, the Serpent's Run specialises in non-bloodsports like dog and horse racing, decathlons, and magic displays. However, the late-summer rodeos sponsored by the Matador's Lodge are also held here.

The arena is named for the carving of a gigantic serpent that runs around the upper rim of the arena, a tribute to the serpent slain by Alcaydian Indros. It is run by Master of Games Jorston "Axetongue" Droaeb.

The Seven Stelae is a collection of massive pillars located between Shiman-Sekh and the port city of Totra in Osirion. The red granite pillars bear the symbols of Pharaoh An-Hepsu I. Several doors and chambers in the pillars have been found.

Seven's Sawmill is the oldest of the six major sawmills on Kyver's Islet in Magnimar and has never changed ownership. Production occurs here, but not in significant volumes, because it is used as the meeting place of the secret society called the Brothers of the Seven. Few know of the group or the meetings.

The Shay Citadels is a gnome monastery established on the island of Irrere, in the Ironbound Archipelago of the Steaming Sea, soon after their exile from the First World. It is located at the base of a waterfall next to the town of Irrere.

The Citadels consist of a number of towers with twisted spires of multi-coloured glass that rise above the waterfall's mists. The monastery was established for research and meditation, and is a regular destination for gnome pilgrims, especially those suffering from the Bleaching.

The monastery sometimes temporarily phases out of existence. Upon returning, all of its buildings remain intact but no gnomes except for the Bleachlings return with it.

Shotep-Kara on the Scorpion Coast of Osirion was a military and commercial hub connecting Tumen to the Obari Ocean. The port city lost its prominent role after the death of the Four Pharaohs and the ruin of Tumen. A number of earthquakes and tsunamis finally drew the city into the sea in 1687 AR.

Shotep-Kara's ancient monuments and temples are now part of the underwater ruins, and the place remains imbued with powerful magic. At the height of Four Pharaohs' reign, conjuration magic connected the port to the far corners of the empire and beyond. The magic is believed to persist to this day, though in wildly unstable form. Oceanic horrors and unquiet spirits infest the waters, but Shotep-Kara might still offer magical passage to faraway places, or even to other planes.

The Shouk is a tribe of degenerate ogrekin that once were human holy men. Their lair is in the Barrier Wall mountains, roughly south-east of the Thuvian city of Duwwor. Once the Shouk were holy men and oracles of some renown. Their wisdom was sought by many other nomadic tribes. However, at some point in the distant past, this clan of holy men became tainted by the blood of ogres and ogrekin began to be born among the ranks of the Shouk. With each generation the taint spread, and more and more brutish, vile ogrekin were born. Now the tribe is composed exclusively of ogrekin, and has lost all prophetic ability. Purely out of tradition, the tribes of the area still occasionally seek out the Shouk in times of great need. These visits rarely yield any result other than the loss of life, as the Shouk are also now cannibals.

The Shrike Cascade is a waterfall on the Shrike River situated about a dozen miles downriver from the Crooked Falls. Here, the Shrike drops from a slippery 50-foot-high cliff, which offers an excellent view of the surrounding landscape, but makes any travel by boat impossible.

The Shrine of Saint Senex is not dedicated to a deity but instead to the centuries-dead Senex, an oracle who devoted her life to recovering the drowned dead of Nisroch Bay and the Yolubilis River. The shrine is now all but forgotten by the modern world; Saint Senex fell in popularity after the Chelaxian Civil War, lying unremembered on a stretch of swampy coast on Argo Isle, just south of the city of Kintargo. The driftwood shrine is devoted to drowned sailors, including a buried sanctum that is home to the bodies of dozens of unknown drowned. The shrine is shielded from magical divination and has sometimes been used as a secret safe house by Hellknights of the Order of the Torrent. Thanks to its decline in prominence, the shrine's only inhabitants are now a pair of rag-clad oracles.

An abandoned shrine to the goddess Sarenrae exists in the Underbridge district of the Varisian city of Magnimar. It was built in the early days of the settlement by a priest of the goddess named Father Vosten, who felt that the burgeoning community could use the Dawnflower's guidance.

He was approached a few months after its completion by Sczarni thugs who demanded protection money. When he refused to pay and attempted to drive them off, he and his acolytes were killed. Their bodies were buried in the small graveyard behind the shrine.

The shrine has lain abandoned ever since, as no devotee of Sarenrae has come forward to rebuild it.
The Shucked Oyster is a brothel in the Dockway district of Magnimar. Seeshaw Raccas is its madam.

Sicnavier's Lair is a dark chasm located in the Hungry Mountains in eastern Virlych, a grim mountain range in southern Ustalav.

A place haunted by the spirits of evil dragons, Sicnavier's Lair is always ruled by a sole umbral dragon who had succeeded the previous draconic master. The spoils of this rivalry is an enormous accumulation of wealth, lore, and slaves, more than enough to convince the successor to ignore ghostly whispers, even as the growing number of restless ghosts attempt to drive them insane.

Whichever dragon controls the lair names themselves 'Sicnavier', a title that has passed to no fewer than six dragons over the centuries. Attempts by outsiders to slay the dragon usually fail, but on those rare occasions when an attempt succeeds, a new, younger dragon takes on the mantle for him or herself.

The deepest pits of Sicnavier's Lair connects to the Darklands, although the dragon master and the creatures below have limited interaction with one another.

As of 4711 AR the ruler was Sicnavier VI, a very old umbral dragon who is said to have been driven to near madness from the tormenting whispers of its slain kin. Other inhabitants, such as Dark folk, nightwings, and tenebrous worms, also inhabit the lair and are most frequently slaves or pets of their draconic overlord.

Terapasillion (which means "Ziggurat of Night" in the Shadowtongue) is an ancient step pyramid eternally cloaked in shadows that predates Taldan settlement in the region, and is located on the southern North Plains of Ravounel near Nidal. It is rumored to have been built by shadow giants during the Age of Darkness, but was more recently inhabited by an umbral dragon. Despite the fact that the dragon has not been seen in decades, locals still avoid the place with a passion.

Tern Hill is a cavern in the Verduran Forest that collapsed, creating an open, roofless space. Tall trees surround the hill, their canopies blocking the view, while their thick roots are visible inside the cavern. Several tunnels connect the cavern's chambers, making it an easily defensible location.

The Rootweft Lodge used the cavern as a base of operations during the Wildwood Revolt, taking advantage of its defensive strengths.

The Thanegraves, located in western Wintercrux in Irrisen, are the final resting place of the Djurstor Confederacy thanes. Two boreal ogre tribes claim the hilly stretch, which is dotted with burial mounds and tombs and largely forgotten by the area's Ulfen folk.

Thanesrest Mausoleum, on the island of Orthost in the Linnorm Kingdom of Broken Bay, is a Thassilonian building that was turned into a mausoleum for kings of Broken Bay. This practice was halted after the body of King Kernic Quicktongue rose up during his funeral, after being possessed of a demon, to murder then create undead of all the mourners.

It is the greatest mausoleum on the isle and was not originally built to inter the dead. The elaborate mausoleum may have originally served as a Thassilonian castle or prison. Its chambers below extend for many miles deep under Orthost.

The Conservatory is a secret, Jalmeri training school for spies located on the inaccessible island of Grand Sarret in the Obari Ocean.

Housed in a collection of buildings that once served to house the harem of the legendary Maharajah Khiben-Sald, the Conservatory trains students in the arts of seduction, cooking, music, and the careful manipulation and influence of governments around the world. Once they complete their training, the graduates are sent to courts, noble houses, and seats of power throughout the Inner Sea region in order to further the ambitions and interests of the government of Jalmeray. They report back to the Conservatory on a regular basis, where their information is catalogued in an extensive, ever-growing library on the island, one of the greatest repositories of secret knowledge throughout the Inner Sea region.

The Three Furies is the name given to the three highest peaks of a mountain range in the center of the Isle of Terror. A storm of void energy surrounds the mountains, making it impossible to see the peaks from the base. However, if one passes through the clouds, the summits can be seen rising to great heights. From the top, one can observe the Wizard-King's Pit at the epicenter of the range, filled with the most horrific creatures on Golarion.

The Uskheart is the central redoubt of the infamous Nidalese circle of druids called the Shades of the Uskwood. Located in the southern Uskwood, the Shades have recently uncovered a disturbing life form known as the hive. These creatures consume and transform everything they encounter, and they seem to be outside the natural order of Golarion. Despite their uniqueness, they are not extraplanar or supernatural in origin and refer to themselves by an unpronounceable Aklo word.

Uddo was the capital of the Teikoku Shogunate in what is now known as Minkai. It was conquered and razed by the armies of Kuga Setsuna during the Battle of Eight Bridges in 6116 IC; Minkai was founded three years later, with the downriver port city of Kasai as its capital.
The Uldani Necropolis is a site in the desert interior of Thuvia. It lies not far from the city of Pashow.

Ulduvai was the last of the Shory flying cities in Garund and the Inner Sea region. In a misguided effort to restore the Shory Empire, the sorcerers of Ulduvai consorted with the powers of the Dark Tapestry, which ended up with the entire population being driven mad by a shoggoth servant of Azathoth. The city crashed upon Mirrshock Glacier in the southeastern Mwangi Expanse, south of Mount Dowama in the Shattered Range in -507 AR.

Three ancient Shory sorcerers survived to the present, albeit as undead creatures: Yakmar, the insane adventurer-mage; Yithdul, the arcane diplomat; and Banderak, former head of the city's aeromantic corps.

The central city of Undarin in Sarkoris is located on the Sarkora River at its confluence with the dry bed of the Rolling River, and was once a center for the selling of livestock and ore in the kingdom of Sarkoris. Once the city was overrun by demons after the opening of the Worldwound in 4606 AR, its stockyards and animal pens were used to temporarily control the human population before being sent off for further processing. Like most of the cities in the region, the holy warriors of the Fifth Mendevian Crusade deemed Undarin to be too corrupted to be rebuilt, and instead razed the city in 4718 AR.

Undervale is a way station in the Umbral Basin that also serves as a safe haven for the Bellflower Network. The area is teeming with predators and potential trouble, but it also offers an opportunity for those seeking to escape Cheliax or Nidal. Constructs and illusions protect the station, and part of it is built underground. Its residents are mostly halflings whose cheerful and welcoming nature is a warm welcome.

The leader of Undervale is undoubtedly Landry Tethertine, who maintains the constructs and seeks new members to join the station.

Located in the Arcadian Ocean west of Acisazi and northwest of the Dismal Nitch, the entrance to the underwater cave known as Urchin's Maw gapes from the side of the continental shelf, overlooking a precipitous drop into the inky depths below. It earns its name from the riot of sizable deep sea urchins that dwell on the rock face surrounding the cave entrance.

Urfa-Halij is an ancient memorial located in the Sophra Prefecture and is sacred to both the people of Taldor and their neighbouring rivals in Qadira. Urfa-Halij marks the location of the first ever battle between the people of Taldor and, as it was at the time, the Empire of Kelesh. Known as the Battle of Urfa to the Taldans and the Battle of Halij to the Qadirans, Urfa-Halij recognises both perspectives and stands as a testament of respect for those soldiers lost by both sides of the war. Both sides recognise this place as sacred and, by custom, no sort of conflict takes place for miles around the monument. This has made it one of the most peaceful and fertile areas along the war-ravaged border between Taldor and Qadira, with rabbits being particularly common.

The Urfa-Halij monument itself consists of two raised arms, one wielding a Taldan-styled longsword, the other a Qadiran scimitar, the blades of both meeting in the middle where a huge diamond is set. This diamond catches even the dimmest of light, making it seem as if the blades are sparking even on a moonlit night. Many debate, due to the striking nature of Urfa-Halij, if it is really a monument to the dead or if, in fact, it is secretly a tribute to the glory of war.

The Usher's Hall is one of the most important buildings in the city of Magnimar as it is home to the Council of Ushers, which is one of Magnimar's ruling bodies. Located in the Capital District, the governmental hub of Magnimar, the Usher's Hall sits opposite the famous Indros cul Vydrarch monument at the centre of the district.

The Usher's Hall is an impressive building, covered with ornate sculptures (as befitting such an important building in the City of Monuments). The building contains enough space to house all 117 members of the Council of Ushers.

The Vaishau Ruin is the grey, scorched remains of an old fortress located in the foothills of the Hungry Mountains of Lastwall, just a stone's throw from the Ustalavic county of Virlych. The ruin stands on the banks of the Mesmos River, which cuts the main pass through the mountains and connects Vigil with Gallowspire.

Valas's Gift was a small town in the north of the nation of Mendev, located on the road north from Kenabres. Although it was populated as of 4711 AR, the town was nothing more than overgrown ruins by 4725 AR, and all that remained were bubbling springs of blood-red water said to be the fertilizing blessing of Valas, a holy man.
Kaleb Valdemar, son of the late Ethram Valdemar and head of House Valdemar, runs Valdemar Villa in Magnimar when not visiting Sandpoint.

The Vale of Green Spears is a forest of massive bamboo plants the size of redwood trees, planted 1,000 years ago according to legends by a wizard challenged by Tian Shen] to plant 100 million trees. The reasons for the challenge vary by the legend's telling and include a test of the wizard's abilities and an attempt to house displaced birds.

Harvesting the sought-after bamboo risks becoming the prey of the progeny of the late forest dragon Genseirin, who laired here and had long protected the old-growth stalks at the forest's center.

The Vale of Shadows is a dangerous valley located in the Five Kings Mountains, in which one of Zon-Kuthon's Star Towers is located. Here the planar fabric between the Netherworld and Golarion is weak, and the Vale all but appears to be within the Netherworld itself, and creatures and monsters from that plane live within. The elves of Elistia forbid entrance to the Vale of Shadows, believing nothing good comes from it and knowing few who venture inside return alive.

The Valley of Shards is a long shallow valley near the headwaters of the Asp River in Osirion. The valley's floor is full of shards of more than a thousand pots. The shards are inscribed with Ancient Osiriani hieroglyphs which seem to deal with magic, both divine and arcane. A small group of hermits study the shards, attempting to piece them together into a coherent message. The kholo roaming the region either have not noticed the hermits or have left them unharmed on purpose.

The shards are inscribed with Ancient Osiriani hieroglyphs which seem to deal with magic, both divine and arcane. A small group of hermits study the shards, attempting to piece them together into a coherent message. The kholo roaming the region either have not noticed the hermits or have left them unharmed on purpose.

Nel-Shevotha is an isolated, reclusive settlement in the foothills of the World's Edge Mountains, inhabited by the undead remains of colonists of the ancient Azlanti Empire who died during Earthfall. The name "Nel-Shevotha" has been lost to history, and in modern Golarion the region is called the Valley of the Azlanti or Valley of the Azlant.

The Valley of the Birthing Death is located in the foothills of the Stormspear Mountains in the far north-east of Icemark in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings. The valley looks as if it had been hewed clean out of the mountain and its narrow red walls create a realm of perpetual twilight. The Varki tribes avoid the valley due to the curse of Birthing Death: a curse manifesting as a rapid rejuvenation of the afflicted taking them back to childhood and then oblivion. This curse is not just physical but mental as well; stealing the memories and experience of the victim as they regress. Many have attempted to study or harness this strange effect but they have all succumbed to it, the knowledge required to understand the strange curse is inevitably one of the first things the curse claims.
The Vault of Silver and Ice was a valuable mine in the Irriseni province of Bleakmarch, discovered during the reign of Queen Velikas.
Located half way between the Cyphergate and Maskyr's Island, the Velashu Ferry is run by Grimas Oltedler.

Velecia Nora is an isolated snow-covered fort made of iron and stone in the Irriseni province known as the Verge. When a unit of soldiers demonstrates cowardice or disobedience, it is marched to Velecia Nora where it receives what is known as the Velecian Cruelty. One soldier in each group of ten is selected in random and beaten to the death by his nine comrades. The remaining soldiers form the new unit and return to duty. Necromancers raise the corpses of the killed soldiers and add them to the ranks of the Decimated, a small army of undead soldiers that drill eternally in the wastes southeast of Velecia Nora. The Decimated brigade is led by the graveknight General Fyodor Yelizaveta.

Versade Villa is the headquarters of House Versade, one of the aristocratic families of the Varisian city of Magnimar best known for throwing amazing parties. It is located at the end of a long line of upper-class villas in the Alabaster District, directly beside Valdemar Villa.

As of 4724 AR, Vespam Artisans is one of the larger businesses still in the Old Kintargo district of Kintargo. It serves as both a metalworking workshop and tinkers' guild. Vespam Artisans is owned and operated by Vespasio Vespam, a fifth-generation Kintargan and lifelong resident of Old Kintargo.

Vhunsaub is thought to be an ancient underwater treasury of the Ghol-Gan, and is located among the Cannibal Isles of the Shackles. Many have tried to explore the ruin, but none have progressed past the entrance guarded by a magically compelled sea serpent.

The Victocora Estate was burned down upon the seizure of Kintargo by House Thrune and its installed Lord Mayor, Barzillai Thrune, in the event now known as the Night of Ashes. The fire killed several members of House Victocora, and the manor ruins were never rebuilt. As of 4724 AR, the Silver Council of Kintargo and Rexus Victocora, the last surviving member, planned to convert the site to a memorial park for the Night of Ashes.

Vigil was not only a mighty fortress that drew warriors of good like a beacon, but also the capital of the country of Lastwall. It was a city of music, joy, and faith that came to a terrible end in the summer of 4719 AR when Tar-Baphon escaped from his centuries-long confinement and unleashed the destructive weapon known as the Radiant Fire upon the city, destroying it completely. Its ruined remains now mar the Gravelands.
The Voghul Caverns are winding warrens beneath the center of the Chitterwood in Isger.

The Voinum Estate is located about three miles outside of the village of Voinaris, at the heart of the Voinum Barony in Taldor's Tandak Prefecture. It serves as the ancestral seat of the vaunted House Voinum.

The estate features one of the most extensive wine cellars in northern Taldor, including centuries-old bottles, and the rest of the estate houses secret rooms containing rare recipes and yeast for winemaking drawn from across Avistan.

High in Volfast's Tower stands the undead Volfast Raven-Banner, cursed to forever remain at his post after he drunkenly boasted to Queen Sascha that, if not for his limited mortal life, he would eternally hold back the Frozen Fog itself in the queen's name. Unfortunately for him, she granted his request, and transformed him into his current state. Here he remains until someone can defeat him in single combat. Some say that whoever defeats him would not only free him, but also inherit his cursed post, while others say they would gain a great, hidden wealth somewhere in the tower.

Vonosh was founded in 3314 AR after the Winter War to serve as the provincial capital of Hoarwood. In 3444 AR, Queen Morgannan razed the city to the ground in a storm of fire and ice and executed all residents of Vonosh—regardless of guilt or innocence—for their part in a plot to overthrow her and in retribution for the death of her daughter.

Following the Hoarwood Rebellion in 3445 AR, the Heralds of Summer's Return tried to use the ruins of Vonosh as a base for guerrilla strikes against the new provincial capital city of Hoarwood. The plan failed because of the angry, restless spirits of those who had been executed by the queen. Some of the Heralds did not escape the attack of the spirits inhabiting the ruins.

The Brine Demon was the ship of the Free Captain Avret Kinkarian. Kinkarian and the Brine Demon menaced Desperation Bay and the southeastern Arcadian Ocean, and were part of a scheme to extort the government of Sargava. The ship crashed into a cliffside of Smuggler's Shiv at some point prior to 4710 AR.

The Nightvoice was a ship of exploration in the employ of the Pathfinder Society, with a home port of Almas, Andoran. The ship went missing while returning from a voyage to the coast of southern Garund decades prior to 4710 AR. Its true fate was unknown to most: it had crashed between a pair of rocks off the shore of Silent Island, a small island off the coast of Smuggler's Shiv in Desperation Bay, decades prior to 4710 AR.
Xin-Grafar, the City of Golden Death, is the lost underground treasure city of Tar-Baphon, the Whispering Tyrant, located on the Isle of Terror.

Xopatl Mountain is located at the centre of the Arcadian nation of Xopatl and is all that remains of the mountains that once covered the entire nation. Xopatl was formed after a massive fragment of the Aeon Star struck Arcadia in the Mildanesi Mountains at the start of the Age of Darkness. The hero-gods of Xopatl found this shard and, after throwing off the yoke of the Razatlan Empire, destroyed the mountains to create the area that is modern Xopatl. Xopatl Mountain is the sole remaining mountain, left looming in place at the nation's centre.

Xopatl Mountain is a massive volcano, but the hero-gods enchanted it so that it would never erupt unless given a specific command, the idea being that the hero-gods could use this should Xopatl ever completely fall to invaders. Xopatl Mountain now has 10,000 years of magma built up below it, and none left alive know what the magical command is that would cause Xopatl Mountain to erupt.

Yahuima's Spring is a geyser located in the east of Xopatl, near to the Mildanesi Mountains that form much of the nation's border. It is said that Yahuima's Spring was created when the hero-god Yahuima wept after her lover, a fellow hero-god, ascended to true divinity and her tears created this geyser. To this day, Yahuima's Spring is infused with divine energy and, whenever it erupts, its water causes nearby plants to bloom with miraculous speed. This miracle normally draws visitors when the geyser is most active during the winter season, however recently these eruptions have instead spread a wave of destruction, blighting plants and corrupting animals.

Yan Ulvedro's Fort was built in the earliest days of the nation of Xopatl by the Arcadian hero-god Ulvedro to help defend the nation against its enemies. Located high in the Mildanesi Mountains, Yan Ulvedro's Fort was designed to protect Xopatl from the neighbouring Razatlan Empire and assisted on the few occasions when Xopatl was attacked. As the power of Razatlan waned, Yan Ulvedro's Fort gradually became more akin to a monastery, and pilgrims from both Xopatl and neighbouring Innazpa now visit the sages of Yan Ulvedro's Fort to seek wisdom and learn their unique mystical techniques. These sages can help travellers reach enlightenment by temporarily pulling their soul out of their body.

Yllaldo Castle is a keep built atop a maze of tunnels in southern Xopatl. It is named for the legendary Arcadian hero-god Yllaldo the Manifester, who lived out the last of his years diligently guarding the keep until the end of the Age of Darkness.

Zar Kragnaral is located in the Stormspear Mountains in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings. The legendary black basalt and blue ice fortress is ruled by the so-called Deathless Jarl.

Zar Kragnaral is an imposing structure nearly seven hundred feet in height. Its windows are made of hard ice and its towers are built of black rock. Delicate bridges of ice connect its black towers. The fortress looms atop a low plateau deep in the mountains. The view from its highest towers affords a panorama of the northern Thanelands and Hagreach.

Zhobl is a volcano located in the eastern Zho Mountains of Qadira. The eruptions of Zhobl and its companion Zhonar covered the nearby town of Shadun in ash and dust, forcing its inhabitants to abandon it.

Zhonar is a volcano located in the eastern Zho Mountains of Qadira. The eruptions of Zhonar and its companion Zhobl covered the nearby town of Shadun in ash and dust, forcing its inhabitants to abandon it.

Zhukuo Tegeg is the communal cemetery of the town of Ngozu on the Mgange Cove island of the Shackles. As of 4712 AR, several juju zombies had recently emerged from the eerie cemetery. Chief wendifa Ajuoga Baas put down many of these undead monsters but sought aid from outsiders to end the zombie menace. Some of Ajuoga's followers thus began to doubt her worthiness as chief wendifa.

Zunderwal Hold is a long-fallen dwarven fortress in the Stained Peaks near Avistan's approach to the Crown of the World. It has long been occupied by bugbears, who fight an ongoing battle for supremacy over who should hold the title of "Huntmaster of the Hold".

Zura's Mouth is a series of springs and pools in the Kaava Lands and the source of the slow and broad Oubinga River which meanders through the jungles of the southwestern Mwangi Expanse before emptying into Desperation Bay. A number of tribes worship the place and use the pools for ritual sacrifice by drowning. The bodies don't last long, as the Oubinga's upper reaches are home to crimson maka-yika, a schooling, carnivorous fish similar to piranhas.
